t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Card network identifier. Active brands: 1=Visa, 2=MasterCard, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro. Inactive: 0=None, 4=Amex, 5=FirePay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China UnionPay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital, 18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Card network identifier. Active brands (IsActive=1 as of 2019): 0=None (unknown/fallback), 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners. Inactive: 4=Amex, 5=Fire Pay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 8=Maestro (active in production today), 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local Credit Card, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China Union Pay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Active set rewritten to DWH snapshot state (Maestro moved active→inactive, None moved inactive→active). Brand names reformatted: MasterCard→Master Card, FirePay→Fire Pay, China UnionPay→China Union Pay, UK Local→UK Local Credit Card. IDs 18-31 dropped entirely.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Card brand name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Card brand name. DDL note: column has a typo ("Car" instead of "Card") — historical artifact from legacy DWH SQL Server migration. Key values: Visa, Master Card, MasterCard, Diners, Amex, American Express, Maestro, Discover, China Union Pay.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped unique constraint reference, dropped usage context (payment UI, transaction records, fraud reporting). Replaced with DWH-specific typo notes and value list not in upstream.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default). Only 4 of 32 are currently active.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Whether this card brand was accepted for deposits as of the 2019 migration snapshot: 1=active, 0=inactive. DWH note: production uses bit type; DWH uses int. This snapshot may not reflect current production acceptance (e.g., Maestro/ID=8 is active in production but shows 0 here).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Changed temporality (currently→as of 2019). Dropped DEFAULT 1 semantics. Dropped 'Only 4 of 32 are currently active.' Added DWH-specific cast and snapshot notes not in upstream.

issues.problem: Upstream active set {1=Visa, 2=MasterCard, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro} rewritten to DWH snapshot state {0=None, 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners}. Brand names reformatted (MasterCard→Master Card, FirePay→Fire Pay, China UnionPay→China Union Pay). IDs 18-31 dropped. Must quote upstream verbatim then add DWH notes.

Upstream description ('Unique constraint prevents duplicates. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting.') entirely replaced with DWH-specific typo notes. Must quote upstream verbatim first.

Upstream description rewritten: 'currently accepted' changed to 'as of 2019 migration snapshot', DEFAULT 1 semantics dropped, '4 of 32' count dropped. Must quote upstream verbatim then add DWH snapshot notes.
Writer treated Tier 1 as 'adapt upstream meaning to DWH context' instead of 'quote verbatim, then annotate'. All 3 Tier 1 descriptions are contextual rewrites, not inheritance.
Element description presents DWH snapshot values (None=active, Maestro=inactive) as canonical truth rather than quoting upstream active set first and noting DWH divergence as an addendum.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Re-run with: (1) Quote upstream Dictionary.CardType descriptions VERBATIM for CardTypeID, CarTypeName, and IsActive — do not adapt temporality, active sets, or brand name spellings. (2) Append DWH-specific notes (frozen snapshot, type cast, typo) as clearly separated 'DWH note:' addenda AFTER the upstream quote. (3) Restore upstream brand name spellings: MasterCard (not Master Card), FirePay (not Fire Pay), China UnionPay (not China Union Pay), UK Local (not UK Local Credit Card). (4) Restore upstream active set in CardTypeID description: 1=Visa, 2=MasterCard, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro. (5) Include IDs 18-31 from upstream ('18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands') with note that only 0-17 exist in DWH snapshot.
